theme: E4 cycle 9 — the OPTIONAL CONFIRMING-LENS cycle (design-spec per-cycle plan: 155-158 measure/iterate, 159 close). Re-score the 5-item Network-Legibility rubric with FOUR INDEPENDENT adversarial lens agents (not Rosetta self-assessment) to validate the c156/c157 self-scores before the decadal close, then iterate the cheap below-floor lifts the lenses surface. III 7-step, measure+iterate.
register: Tokyo Night dark-first; honest-topology (no fabricated edges; the band-1 fix makes the REAL hub-and-spoke visible rather than inventing structure; the node-color legend decodes the existing class palette, it does not recolor); <=2 fighting colors (cyan --color-link + purple --color-primary; node-legend swatches are the EXISTING .mmd classDef fills, parsed from the chart, not a new accent palette); motion unchanged (the band-1 IO fallback only guarantees the existing compose state, adds no new motion); writing-guidelines AVOID honored (no marketing adjectives; the new copy is plain — 'each box is a vault', 'box colour = the vault's class'); a11y both modes via the permanent gate-4 dark+light loop.
operator_scope: AskUserQuestion at the plan gate: (1) path = c158 CONFIRMING WORKFLOW then STOP (operator reviews the independent scores before authorizing the c159 close); (2) scope = MEASURE + ITERATE (full 7-step). Lens assessment = a multi-agent Workflow (4 parallel adversarial agents), mirroring c155. COMMIT + PUSH, NO Vercel deploy (E4 bundles its deploy at the close).

iii_7step.measure: Machine baseline GREEN before touching anything: git pull already up-to-date (HEAD d827f4b/c157); npx astro build 162pp/0err (built directly to keep the data layer byte-identical — skips the prebuild regen); npm run test:gates 83/83 (reconciled the true count: gate-4 a11y 16 + gate-6 9 + gate-7 5 + gate-8 9 + gate-9 35 + gate-10 perf 5 + gate-11 hero 4 = 83; the c157 narrative's '86' was an overcount). Captured neutral screenshots of /network + /vaults/graph + /vaults/Harness.aDNA at desktop 1440 + mobile 375 and EYEBALLED them (the c154/c155 lesson — render problems are invisible in source). THEN ran a 4-agent lens Workflow (run wf_0a86b406-8ae; 500,970 subagent tokens; 262s) scoring the 5-item rubric independently — each agent given only neutral facts + source paths + screenshots, NOT primed with the self-scores.
iii_7step.orient: Owning-lens-authoritative scores (vs the c157 self-scores in parens): node_clarity 3.5 [Newcomer] (self 4.75); edge_honesty 4.5 [Diagram-Reviewer] (5); local_shared_boundary 5 [Node-Operator] (5); navigability 3.0 [IA] (4.75); join_scent 4.0 [Node-Operator] (4.5). TWO items BELOW the GREEN floor of 4, exactly mirroring the c155 outcome where independence found gaps a self-assessment over-rated: (a) navigability 3.0 — the IA's 'cardinal defect': the entire /vaults + /vaults/graph + /vaults/[slug] subsystem (40+ pages) has NO top-nav entry and never lights an active state; the deep surfaces are one-way cul-de-sacs with no link back to /network; plus mobile-graph illegibility + a right-third desktop layout cram. (b) node_clarity 3.5 — the Newcomer's owned gaps: the keystone 'node = Home.aDNA + its vaults' evaporates on the deep surfaces; the graph renders ~14 vault-class fill colours with NO node-color legend (an undecoded rainbow); Home.aDNA sits anonymously in the orphan box. ALSO a cross-cutting MAJOR flagged by 3 of 4 lenses (Diagram-Rev/Node-Op/Newcomer): the /network band-1 'diagram-as-proof' NetworkDiagram renders empty (only faint spokes, no nodes/labels/hub) — the IntersectionObserver compose entrance stranded armed-hidden when the band never reaches 0.35 visibility (a real robustness gap: the no-JS + reduced-motion paths compose fine, but a JS+motion user who doesn't scroll the band into view, or a short viewport, sees the marquee proof blank).
iii_7step.select: Cheap copy/CSS/markup lifts for the two BELOW-floor items + the 3-lens band-1 bug, deferring heavy/structural/data items to c159. (N1) navigability — add a 'The Lattice' top-nav entry -> /vaults (the IA cardinal blocker; gives the 40-page subsystem a persistent way-in + active state via startsWith('/vaults'); naming matches the index H1 + breadcrumbs). (N2) navigability — add a '<- Network' back-crumb to /vaults/graph + /vaults/[slug] (closes the one-way cul-de-sac so the node definition + join-scent on /network are reachable from the deep surfaces). (C1) node_clarity — band-1 NetworkDiagram IO fallback: a setTimeout that forces the composed state after 1.6s so the marquee proof is always visible even if IO never fires (idempotent; a real scroll-in still plays the entrance). (C2) node_clarity — graph lede restates 'each box is a vault; an aDNA computer (a node) is a Home.aDNA plus the vaults that live on it, coloured below by vault class' + a 'New here? Start with what the network is ->' link to /network. (C3) node_clarity — a data-driven NODE-COLOR LEGEND on /vaults/graph (Newcomer's owned major): the classDef palette is parsed straight from the .mmd so swatches match the rendered boxes 1:1; the present classes are censused + labelled (Platform 10, Org-Vault 8, Forge 7, Framework 3, Org-Graph 3, + 9 singletons). (E1) edge_honesty polish — /network .le-super made a thicker dash so supersedes reads apart from companion (matches /vaults/graph; the legend stops implying 5 unique line styles).
iii_7step.implement: 5 files. site/src/components/common/Header.astro — added {label:'The Lattice', href:'/vaults'} (N1) + bumped the desktop-nav breakpoint 768px->900px (the 7th item overflowed the row at the 768 switch-on — caught by gate-9, see re_measure). site/src/pages/vaults/graph.astro — '<- Network' crumb (N2); node-clarity lede line + /network link (C2); node-color legend block parsed from the .mmd classDefs with a census + labels + swatches (C3). site/src/pages/vaults/[slug].astro — '<- Network' crumb + breadcrumb link styling (N2). site/src/components/sections/NetworkDiagram.astro — setTimeout(data-in, 1600) IO fallback (C1; benefits /network AND the homepage where the component is shared). site/src/pages/network.astro — .le-super thicker dash (E1). Generator/.mmd/vaults.json UNTOUCHED (the node-color legend reads the existing palette; it does not regenerate).
iii_7step.re_measure: First post-edit gate run = 76/83 — gate-9 caught a REAL regression I introduced: the 7th nav item ('The Lattice') overflowed the desktop nav row at 768px (horizontal overflow on EVERY page, since the header is global). Fixed by raising the .nav-desktop breakpoint 768->900px (tablets <900 get the hamburger, which holds all 7 with no overflow; >=1024 desktop preset shows the full inline nav). Re-run: 83/83 GREEN. build 162pp/0err. axe 0 AA BOTH modes (gate-4 16 dark+light tests). Lighthouse desktop preset all-100: /network 100/100/100/100, /vaults/graph 100/100/100/100 (up from c156's throttled ~89 — the desktop preset confirms the Mermaid layout cost is method-throttling, not a real regression; c159 prerender deferral still stands as robustness-under-throttling), /vaults/Harness.aDNA 100/100/100/100. Eyeballed the re-rendered surfaces: the node-color legend swatches match the graph node fills; both breadcrumb parents render; the band-1 diagram now shows the composed purple hub + labelled spokes (was empty faint spokes at baseline). Data layer byte-identical (git status clean on site/src/data/).
iii_7step.record: this JSON + e4_c158_lens_results.json (raw 4-agent verdicts) + cycle log in the session + mission/STATE/campaign cascade

lens_scores.method: Independent 4-agent Workflow (run wf_0a86b406-8ae; 500,970 subagent tokens; 262s). Owning-lens score authoritative per item. Raw per-agent scores + findings + top_fixes in e4_c158_lens_results.json.

two_below_floor: navigability (3.0, IA — no top-nav entry + one-way cul-de-sac)
node_clarity (3.5, Newcomer — concept evaporates on deep surfaces + no node-color legend)
pre_fix_independent.self_vs_independent_delta: self-scores (c157) node_clarity 4.75 / navigability 4.75 vs independent 3.5 / 3.0 — the confirming pass earned its keep, exactly the c155 lesson (a self-assessment over-rated the two seam dimensions).

post_fix_self_rescore.method: Rosetta self-re-score traceable to the specific findings addressed (NOT a second Workflow — proportionate for the validate step, per the c155 precedent; an independent re-confirmation is offered to the operator at the review gate / c159). node_clarity 3.5->4.5: band-1 composes (3-lens major closed) + graph lede carries the node concept + node-color legend decodes the rainbow; the Home.aDNA-in-orphans tension remains (defensibly honest, minor). edge_honesty 4.5->4.75: supersedes sample now visually distinct on /network; a fully distinct stroke (vs the dashed family) is a c159 generator option. navigability 3.0->4.5: 'The Lattice' nav entry + active state (the IA cardinal blocker) + '<- Network' back-crumb (cul-de-sac closed); mobile-graph illegibility + the desktop right-third layout are heavier items carried to c159. join_scent held at 4.0: untouched this cycle (the get-started node-onboarding seam — the Node-Operator's main finding — is the deeper content fix, carried to c159). NO item < 4 -> the rubric clears the GREEN floor.

change_summary: site/src/components/common/Header.astro — N1: added 'The Lattice' -> /vaults nav entry (the IA cardinal navigability blocker — the 40-page vault/graph subsystem had no global way-in and never lit an active state; startsWith('/vaults') now lights it on the index/graph/detail; naming matches the /vaults index H1 + breadcrumbs). Bumped the desktop-nav breakpoint 768->900px so the 7th item does not overflow the row at the switch-on (tablets <900 get the hamburger).

site/src/pages/vaults/graph.astro — N2: '<- Network' back-crumb (+ .crumb-network style) closing the one-way cul-de-sac. C2: a node-clarity lede line ('Each box is a vault; an aDNA computer — a node — is a Home.aDNA plus the vaults that live on it, coloured below by vault class') + a 'New here? Start with what the network is ->' /network link (underlined for link-in-text-block). C3: a node-color legend — classDef colours parsed from the imported .mmd (drift-free), the present classes censused + labelled, rendered as swatch+label chips under a 'Box colour = the vault's class' subhead (the edge legend gets a matching 'Line style = the kind of relationship' subhead).

site/src/pages/vaults/[slug].astro — N2: '<- Network' back-crumb alongside '<- The Lattice' (+ .vault-breadcrumb a / .crumb-network styling) so the deep vault page has both parents (registry + narrative).

site/src/components/sections/NetworkDiagram.astro — C1: a setTimeout(data-in, 1600) fallback after io.observe so the band-1 'diagram-as-proof' always composes even if the 0.35 IntersectionObserver threshold is never met (short viewport / anchor landing / no scroll). 3-of-4 lenses flagged the marquee proof rendering empty. Idempotent — a real scroll-in still plays the entrance. Shared component, so the homepage band benefits too.

site/src/pages/network.astro — E1: .le-super made a thicker dash (border-top-width:3px) so supersedes reads apart from companion's thin dash, matching /vaults/graph and the .mmd long-dash linkStyle (Diagram-Reviewer: the legend over-implied 5 unique line styles).

doctrine_compliance.honest_topology: STRENGTHENED — the band-1 fix makes the REAL hub-and-spoke visible (it was rendering blank), it invents no structure; the node-color legend decodes the EXISTING class palette without recolouring; the '<- Network' crumb + lede link surface the honest node definition on the deep surfaces; ZERO fabricated edges; generator/.mmd/vaults.json untouched.
doctrine_compliance.two_fighting_colors: PASS — node-legend swatches are the existing .mmd classDef fills (the graph already draws them), parsed from the chart, not a NEW accent palette competing with cyan/purple; all new chrome (crumbs, lede link, legend subheads) uses cyan --color-link.
doctrine_compliance.motion_budget: PASS — the band-1 IO fallback guarantees the EXISTING compose state; it adds no new motion and is reduced-motion-safe (the reduce branch returns before arming, so it stays composed).
doctrine_compliance.no_marketing_adjectives: PASS — new copy is plain and functional ('each box is a vault', 'box colour = the vault's class', 'New here? Start with what the network is').
doctrine_compliance.dual_audience: PASS — the newcomer gets the node concept restated on the deep surface + the colour key + a back-link to the explainer; the IA/operator gets a persistent nav entry + active state + the bidirectional crumb.
doctrine_compliance.engine_files_unedited: PASS — skill_iii_cycle / skill_site_design_pipeline / skill_decadal_aar / the runners / the ADR-023 generator (build_vaults_data.mjs) + vaults.json + vaults_graph.mmd ALL UNTOUCHED (the node-color legend READS the .mmd palette; it does not regenerate). 5 site files only.
doctrine_compliance.dark_first: PASS — verified both modes; the both-modes axe gate-4 is permanent.
doctrine_compliance.a11y_mechanism: PASS — the node-legend swatches are aria-hidden decorative (the text label carries the meaning); in-text links underlined (lede-node link); axe 0 AA both modes on all surfaces; the new nav item carries the same active/hover semantics as the existing items.

validation.build: PASS — astro build 162 pages, 0 errors; no route change; generator/data untouched (no prebuild churn; built via npx astro build to keep the data layer byte-identical).
validation.playwright_gates: 83/83 PASS. First post-edit run was 76/83 — gate-9 caught a self-introduced 768px horizontal-overflow on every page (the 7th nav item overflowing the desktop row at the switch-on); fixed by raising the .nav-desktop breakpoint to 900px -> 83/83 GREEN. (Note: the true gate count is 83, not the c157 narrative's 86.)
validation.axe_wcag_aa: 0 violations BOTH modes (gate-4 dark+light loop, 16 tests) on the audited surfaces including /network + /vaults/graph + a vault detail.

lighthouse.graph: Perf 100 / A11y 100 / BP 100 / SEO 100 (desktop preset; up from c156's throttled ~89 — confirms the Mermaid layout cost is method-throttling; the build-time prerender is a c159 robustness item, not a regression)

validation.functional: PASS — 'The Lattice' nav entry lights active on /vaults/graph + /vaults/[slug] (startsWith); both breadcrumb parents render on the deep surfaces; the node-color legend swatches match the rendered node fills (parsed from the same .mmd); the band-1 NetworkDiagram composes (purple hub + labelled spokes visible) where the baseline showed empty faint spokes.

network_legibility_confirmation.verdict: The confirming-lens pass found TWO below-floor items (navigability 3.0, node_clarity 3.5) the c157 self-scores had rated 4.75 — the independence earned its keep. Both lifted off the floor this cycle with cheap nav/copy/CSS/markup. Post-fix self-rescore: no item < 4 (mean 4.55) -> rubric clears GREEN. An INDEPENDENT re-confirmation (a second 4-agent Workflow on the post-fix surfaces) is offered to the operator at the review gate or folded into c159.
network_legibility_confirmation.1_node_clarity: 3.5 -> 4.5 — band-1 composes (marquee proof visible); graph lede restates node = Home.aDNA + its vaults + links /network; node-color legend decodes the class palette. Carried: a Home.aDNA-in-orphans callout (minor; defensibly honest).
network_legibility_confirmation.2_edge_honesty: 4.5 -> 4.75 — /network supersedes sample visually distinct from companion. Carried (c159 generator option): a genuinely distinct supersedes stroke vs the dashed family.
network_legibility_confirmation.3_local_shared_boundary: 5 (held) — exemplary; the two-column boundary + step-3 transmission-unit copy unchanged.
network_legibility_confirmation.4_navigability: 3.0 -> 4.5 — 'The Lattice' nav entry + active state (the IA cardinal blocker) + '<- Network' back-crumb (cul-de-sac closed). Carried (c159): mobile-graph illegibility (swap to the keyboard node-twin list / tap-to-zoom at <=480px) + the desktop right-third layout cram (generator/Mermaid).
network_legibility_confirmation.5_join_scent: 4.0 (held) — on-page join-scent strong; the deeper fix (the 'Run your first node' CTA + step-1 promise a Home.aDNA node interview, but /get-started covers only PROJECT onboarding — no Home.aDNA/interview/federation) is a content seam carried to c159.

deferred_to_159: Independent RE-CONFIRMATION of the post-fix lifts (a second 4-agent Workflow) — proportionate to fold into the close, or run at the operator's request; the c158 validate used a traceable self-rescore per the c155 precedent.

Mobile-graph illegibility (Node-Op + IA + Newcomer): at <=480px the 40-node Mermaid scales to a sub-pixel-label thumbnail; surface the already-built keyboard node-twin list as the visible default, or a tap-to-zoom/pannable container. Medium/heavy.

/vaults/graph desktop layout: the connected component is crammed into the right third with a large empty left gutter + long thin cross-width edges; constrain max-width / center / compact the direction. Generator/Mermaid (heavy).

Orphan-cluster visual hierarchy (IA): the 19-node 'Not yet linked' row paints ABOVE and outweighs the 22-edge connected subgraph; reorder so the real relationships read first. Generator/.mmd emission (heavy).

get-started node-onboarding seam (Node-Operator, the join_scent main finding): the 'Run your first node ->' CTA + step-1 promise a Home.aDNA node interview the /get-started destination does not deliver (it covers project onboarding only). Either add a node-bootstrap section to /get-started or align the /network step-1 copy. Content (medium).

Distinct supersedes stroke vs the dashed companion family (Diagram-Reviewer): a different arrowhead or dotted-vs-dashed pattern so 'same shape = same concept' holds without relying on the weight delta. Generator/.mmd.

E4 decadal CLOSE (cycle 159): AAR + full Lighthouse + STATE/STR cascade; ADR-033 (the network_edges.yaml overlay / adaptation seam) ratifies at the phase-exit gate per campaign SO #14; the homepage DNA-helix hero + the whole E4 surface deploy with the close bundle.
deploy: NOT deployed — commit + push only (operator standing instruction; the E4 surface bundles its deploy at the c159 close). #needs-human: rotate SS_VERCEL_TOKEN (recurring CLI-stdout leak; carry-forward).

aar_lightweight.worked: Running the confirming lenses as an independent multi-agent Workflow (not self-assessment) again found exactly the gaps a self-score missed — navigability 3.0 and node_clarity 3.5 vs the c157 self 4.75 — the SAME pattern c155 established, now reproduced on the post-build surfaces. Eyeballing the actual rendered surfaces (the c154/c155 lesson) was again essential: the band-1 empty-diagram and the undecoded node-colour rainbow are invisible in source and in element-presence gates.
aar_lightweight.didnt: A confirming/iterate cycle STILL introduced a regression (the 7th nav item overflowed the 768px desktop row on every page) — caught only by gate-9, not by eye. A quick mental check of 'does a new nav item fit at the switch-on width' would have pre-empted it.
aar_lightweight.finding: The two below-floor items were both SEAM defects — not failures inside a surface, but failures BETWEEN surfaces (no nav entry, one-way cul-de-sacs, the node concept not carried onto the deep pages, the marquee proof stranded by an observer). Self-assessment scored each surface in isolation and missed the seams; the independent IA + Newcomer lenses, reading across surfaces, found them. The cheap fixes were correspondingly cross-surface (nav, crumbs, a restated lede, a shared-component fallback).
aar_lightweight.change: When adding a global-nav item, re-check the desktop-nav switch-on width in the same edit (the breakpoint is a load-bearing constraint, not just styling).
aar_lightweight.follow_up: c159 decadal close: optionally re-run the 4-lens Workflow on the post-fix surfaces for an independent GREEN confirmation; then AAR + full Lighthouse + ADR-033 ratify + the carried mobile-graph + layout + get-started-seam items + the bundled deploy.

notes: The optional confirming-lens cycle did its job: independent adversarial lenses re-scored the rubric and broke the c157 self-assessment on the two SEAM dimensions (navigability 4.75->3.0, node_clarity 4.75->3.5), reproducing the c155 finding that independence catches what self-scoring over-rates. Both below-floor items were lifted to 4.5 with cheap cross-surface fixes (a 'The Lattice' nav entry + active state, '<- Network' back-crumbs, a node-clarity lede + a data-driven node-color legend, and a band-1 IO fallback that makes the marquee proof reliably visible), the green suite held (83/83, axe 0 AA both modes, Lighthouse all-100), and the generator/data layer stayed byte-identical. The cycle also re-proved that a measure/iterate pass can still ship a regression (the nav overflow) — caught here by gate-9. Heavier structural items (mobile-graph legibility, the desktop layout, the orphan hierarchy, the get-started node-onboarding seam) are deliberately carried to the c159 decadal close, where an independent re-confirmation can fold in.
